---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables
  group_var: "treatment_group"
  before_var: "baseline_score"
  after_var: "endline_score"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  treatment_label: "Treatment"
  control_label: "Control"
  metric_name: "Standardized Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Program Impact: Dumbbell Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Global chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 3.5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only in the columns essential for this visualization
  drop_na(!!sym(params$group_var), !!sym(params$before_var), !!sym(params$after_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub and standardize text (fixes trailing spaces, case inconsistencies)
  mutate(
    Clean_Group = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$group_var)))),
    
    # Intelligently map messy data to the clean Policy Labels defined in YAML
    # and wrap text to 15 characters so long labels don't ruin the Y-axis margin
    Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Group, "(?i)Treat|Intervention|1|Yes") ~ str_wrap(params$treatment_label, width = 15),
      TRUE ~ str_wrap(params$control_label, width = 15)
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Efficiently calculate means grouped by our clean labels
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarize(
    Before = mean(!!sym(params$before_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    After  = mean(!!sym(params$after_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. Reshape data structurally for ggplot
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Before, After),
    names_to = "Time",
    values_to = "Score"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Time = factor(Time, levels = c("Before", "After"))
  )
```


```{r plot-dumbbell}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

# Isolate data to attach labels strictly to the top line (acting as a clean legend)
label_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(Group == str_wrap(params$treatment_label, width = 15))

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Score, y = Group)) +
  
  # Draw the connecting line between Before and After
  geom_line(aes(group = Group), color = cerp_cols["neutral"], linewidth = 2) +
  
  # Draw the Before and After dots
  geom_point(aes(color = Time), size = 5) +
  
  # Add "Before" and "After" text directly above the top dots
  geom_text(data = label_data, aes(label = Time, color = Time), 
            vjust = -2, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  
  # Map CERP colors
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Before" = unname(cerp_cols["neutral"]), 
    "After"  = unname(cerp_cols["primary"])
  )) +
  
  # Expand Y limits slightly so the text labels aren't cut off at the top edge
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.2, 0.4))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip unnecessary legends
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Impact Shift:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("Comparing overall averages before and after the program."),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (params$metric_name),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
